perturbation.original_text: Non -Disclosure Agreement This Agreement is entered into as of _______________, ____ between __________________________ (“Recipient”) and the International Code of Conduct for Private Security Service Providers’ Association (the “ICoCA” or the “Association ”)(together the Parties). Primary Purpose The Primary Purpose of the Agreement is to enable the sharing of information between the Association and the Recipient in a manner that enables the proper functioning of the Association and the full participation of members while also providing access to Confidential Information (as defined in this Agreement) and the responsibilities to ensure that such information is not disseminated or used inappropriately.
perturbation.changed_text: Non -Disclosure Agreement This Agreement is entered into as of _______________, ____ between __________________________ (“User”) and the International Code of Conduct for Private Security Service Providers’ Association (the “ICoCA” or the “Association ”)(together the Parties). Primary Purpose The Primary Purpose of the Agreement is to enable the sharing of information between the Association and the User in a manner that enables the proper functioning of the Association and the full participation of members while also providing access to Confidential Information (as defined in this Agreement) and the responsibilities to ensure that such information is not disseminated or used inappropriately.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Recipient' to 'User' in the introduction without updating subsequent references creates confusion. The document later refers to 'Recipient' and its responsibilities, creating doubt about whether 'User' and 'Recipient' are the same entity. This introduces ambiguity regarding the agreement's application.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Recipient hereby agrees and affirms his/her understanding of the following: (1) that in the normal course of business of the Association, there may be times when Recipient, as a Director, Officer, or Staff Member of the Association, receives information that is confidential, sensitive, and/or not in the public domain and that, if disclosed to persons other than those for whom the information as intended, has the potential to harm the Association, its individual members, other participants, or any of their business partners or related entities or persons (such information her eafter referred to as “Confidential Information”); (2) that the disclosure of Confidential Information to persons other than those to whom the information was intended, and for purposes other than the normal business operations of the Association, is not only damaging to the fundamental relationship of trust that exists between and among the Association and its members but may jeopardize the Association’s ability to function effectively and subject it to potential legal liability and other consequences; (3) that the Recipient has been or will be provided with access to certain Confidential Information solely for the purpose of carrying out his/her responsibilities as a Director, Officer, or Staff Member of the Association; (4) that the Recipient shall not disclose Confidential Information to any person other than the Directors, Officers, or Staff Members of the Association, unless he/she has clear instruction from the Executive Director or Board Chairperson to do so, and then shall disclose such information only for the limited purpose for which such instruction is given and no other purpose; (5) that the Recipient is aware of and has reviewed the ICoCA’s Conflict of Interest and Information Security policies and that Recipient shall not disclose Confidential Information to any person within the Association (i.e., the Directors, Officers, or Staff Members) if the disclosure of such information would violate the Association’s Conflict of Interest or Information Security policies; (6) that in the event Recipient violates any part of this Confidentiality and Non-Disclosure Agreement, the Association may pursue legal and/or equitable remedies from Recipient; (7) that in the event any proceeding to enforce this Confidentiality and Non-Disclosure Agreement or decide any matter relating to it Recipient becomes necessary, the Agreement shall be governed by the laws of Switzerland pertaining thereto and the Recipient agrees to submit to the non-exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of the Canton of Geneva, Switzerland; and (8) that continued adherence to this Confidentiality and Non-Disclosure Agreement is an essential term of employment as a Staff Member of the Association and/or an essential part of the Recipient’s roles and responsibilities as a Director or Officer, the violation of which will permit the Association to terminate such employment or terminate such person from his7her position in addition to seeking any other legal or equitable remedies.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to the party as 'Recipient,' which is consistent with the contradicted text. The changed text replaces 'Recipient' with 'User' in the agreement's introduction, while the contradicted text still refers to 'Recipient.' This creates a misaligned terminology as defined in the perturbation definitions, leading to confusion and ambiguity about who is bound by the agreement's terms, creating a direct contradiction. Therefore, the change creates a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Parties agree that the disclosure of information designated as Confidential Information under this Confidentiality and Non-Disclosure Agreement shall not breach the Agreement in the following circumstances: (a) Where the Confidential Information is in the public domain for reasons other than any act or omission of the Association or the Recipient; and/or (b) Where the Parties have a mandatory, legally binding obligation which requires disclosure of Confidential Information .
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties agree that the disclosure of information designated as Confidential Data under this Confidentiality and Non-Disclosure Agreement shall not breach the Agreement in the following circumstances: (a) Where the Confidential Data is in the public domain for reasons other than any act or omission of the Association or the Recipient; and/or (b) Where the Parties have a mandatory, legally binding obligation which requires disclosure of Confidential Data .
perturbation.explanation: Switching from 'Confidential Information' to 'Confidential Data' in this section, while retaining 'Confidential Information' in the definition and other clauses, introduces ambiguity. It's unclear if these terms are interchangeable, potentially leading to disputes over what's protected. The original definition section only mentions 'Confidential Information' but not 'Confidential Data'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Definition of Confidential Information For the purposes of this Agreement, Confidential Information includes, but is not limited to, financial or other competitive business information that may be collected by the Association regarding industry members (and non-members) of the Association, information of a personal nature regarding any of th e officers, staff, or employees of the Association or its members, information shared in the course of Board or other Association meetings or discussions (unless such information has been specifically cleared for public release), and any other information which might be reasonably foreseen to be harmful if disclosed outside of the Association and/or for reasons other than in accordance with a D irector’s, Officer’s, or Staff Member ’s duties to the Association. In the case that the Recipient is unclear or has any question regarding whether or not a particular piece of information falls within Confidential Information, the Recipient will consult with the Executive Director or Office Manager prior to disclosing such information.
perturbation.justification: The change replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Confidential Data' in the modified section. The contradicted section defines 'Confidential Information' but does not define 'Confidential Data'. This creates a strong contradiction, as it is unclear whether the definition of 'Confidential Information' applies to 'Confidential Data'. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition, where the contract uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them, leading to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: that in the normal course of business of the Association, there may be times when Recipient, as a Director, Officer, or Staff Member of the Association, receives information that is confidential, sensitive, and/or not in the public domain and that, if disclosed to persons other than those for whom the information as intended, has the potential to harm the Association, its individual members, other participants, or any of their business partners or related entities or persons (such information her eafter referred to as “Confidential Information”);
perturbation.changed_text: that in the normal course of business of the Association, there may be times when Recipient, as a Director, Officer, or Staff Member of the Association, receives material that is confidential, sensitive, and/or not in the public domain and that, if disclosed to persons other than those for whom the material as intended, has the potential to harm the Association, its individual members, other participants, or any of their business partners or related entities or persons (such information her eafter referred to as “Confidential Information”);
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'information' to 'material' here creates uncertainty. The document defines 'Confidential Information', but if 'material' encompasses a broader range of items than 'information', it's unclear what is ultimately protected under the agreement. The definition of 'Confidential Information' remains unchanged, creating a mismatch.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Definition of Confidential Information For the purposes of this Agreement, Confidential Information includes, but is not limited to, financial or other competitive business information that may be collected by the Association regarding industry members (and non-members) of the Association, information of a personal nature regarding any of th e officers, staff, or employees of the Association or its members, information shared in the course of Board or other Association meetings or discussions (unless such information has been specifically cleared for public release), and any other information which might be reasonably foreseen to be harmful if disclosed outside of the Association and/or for reasons other than in accordance with a D irector’s, Officer’s, or Staff Member ’s duties to the Association. In the case that the Recipient is unclear or has any question regarding whether or not a particular piece of information falls within Confidential Information, the Recipient will consult with the Executive Director or Office Manager prior to disclosing such information.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'information' to 'material' broadens the scope of what the Recipient receives. The 'Contradicted Text' defines 'Confidential Information', implying specific types of information are covered. By using 'material' in the 'Changed Text', it's unclear whether the definition of 'Confidential Information' still applies or if a broader range of 'material' is now subject to confidentiality, creating uncertainty and a potential contradiction as to what is actually considered 'Confidential Information'. This is because the 'Perturbation Explanation' specifically states that the definition of 'Confidential Information' remains unchanged, creating a mismatch.

perturbation.changed_text: that the Recipient shall not disclose Confidential Information to any person other than the Directors, Officers, or Staff Members of the Association, unless he/she has clear instruction from the Executive Director or Board Chairperson to do so, and then shall disclose such data only for the limited purpose for which such instruction is given and no other purpose; (5) that the Recipient is aware of and has reviewed the ICoCA’s Conflict of Interest and Information Security policies and that Recipient shall not disclose Confidential Information to any person within the Association (i.e., the Directors, Officers, or Staff Members) if the disclosure of such information would violate the Association’s Conflict of Interest or Information Security policies;
perturbation.explanation: Using 'Confidential Information' and 'data' interchangeably in the same paragraph introduces ambiguity. It is unclear whether 'data' is a subset of 'Confidential Information' or something else altogether. This change makes the obligations regarding disclosure unclear, specifically in this section where it references both.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Recipient hereby agrees and affirms his/her understanding of the following: (1) that in the normal course of business of the Association, there may be times when Recipient, as a Director, Officer, or Staff Member of the Association, receives information that is confidential, sensitive, and/or not in the public domain and that, if disclosed to persons other than those for whom the information as intended, has the potential to harm the Association, its individual members, other participants, or any of their business partners or related entities or persons (such information her eafter referred to as “Confidential Information”); (2) that the disclosure of Confidential Information to persons other than those to whom the information was intended, and for purposes other than the normal business operations of the Association, is not only damaging to the fundamental relationship of trust that exists between and among the Association and its members but may jeopardize the Association’s ability to function effectively and subject it to potential legal liability and other consequences; (5) that the Recipient is aware of and has reviewed the ICoCA’s Conflict of Interest and Information Security policies and that Recipient shall not disclose Confidential Information to any person within the Association (i.e., the Directors, Officers, or Staff Members) if the disclosure of such information would violate the Association’s Conflict of Interest or Information Security policies; (6) that in the event Recipient violates any part of this Confidentiality and Non-Disclosure Agreement, the Association may pursue legal and/or equitable remedies from Recipient; (7) that in the event any proceeding to enforce this Confidentiality and Non-Disclosure Agreement or decide any matter relating to it Recipient becomes necessary, the Agreement shall be governed by the laws of Switzerland pertaining thereto and the Recipient agrees to submit to the non-exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of the Canton of Geneva, Switzerland; and (8) that continued adherence to this Confidentiality and Non-Disclosure Agreement is an essential term of employment as a Staff Member of the Association and/or an essential part of the Recipient’s roles and responsibilities as a Director or Officer, the violation of which will permit the Association to terminate such employment or terminate such person from his7her position in addition to seeking any other legal or equitable remedies.
perturbation.justification: The original text consistently uses 'Confidential Information.' The changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'data' in subsection (4) while retaining 'Confidential Information' in the other subsections. This introduces ambiguity, as it's unclear whether 'data' and 'Confidential Information' are interchangeable or distinct. This directly contradicts the overall intention of the paragraph which is to provide clear instructions regarding Confidential Information, creating a misaligned terminology, and therefore leading to a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Agreement' to 'Understanding' in clauses concerning legal recourse and terms of employment creates a contradiction. The rest of the document still refers to the 'Agreement,' potentially weakening its legal enforceability, as 'Understanding' implies a less formal arrangement. In this section, it talks about the enforcement of the NDA, and also the consequences of breaching it.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties agree that the disclosure of information designated as Confidential Data under this Confidentiality and Non-Disclosure Agreement shall not breach the Agreement in the following circumstances: (a) Where the Confidential Data is in the public domain for reasons other than any act or omission of the Association or the Recipient; and/or (b) Where the Parties have a mandatory, legally binding obligation which requires disclosure of Confidential Data .
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Agreement' to 'Understanding' introduces misaligned terminology that creates an in-text contradiction. While the original text and the contradicted text consistently refer to a 'Confidentiality and Non-Disclosure Agreement,' the changed text uses 'Confidentiality and Non-Disclosure Understanding' in a section detailing enforcement and consequences of breach. This undermines the clarity of the original agreement, making it unclear if the legal recourse and terms of employment apply to the 'Understanding.' This contradicts the clause referring to the 'Confidentiality and Non-Disclosure Agreement', as 'Understanding' implies a less formal arrangement. The modified text impacts enforceability, creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: For the purposes of this Agreement, Confidential Information includes, but is not limited to, financial or other competitive business information that may be collected by the Association regarding industry members (and non-members) of the Association, information of a personal nature regarding any of th e officers, staff, or employees of the Association or its members, information shared in the course of Board or other Association meetings or discussions (unless such information has been specifically cleared for public release), and any other information which might be reasonably foreseen to be harmful if disclosed outside of the Association and/or for reasons other than in accordance with a D irector’s, Officer’s, or Staff Member ’s duties to the Association. In the case that the Recipient is unclear or has any question regarding whether or not a particular piece of information falls within Confidential Information, the Recipient will consult with the Executive Director or Office Manager prior to disclosing such information.
perturbation.changed_text: For the purposes of this Agreement, Confidential Data includes, but is not limited to, financial or other competitive business information that may be collected by the Association regarding industry members (and non-members) of the Association, information of a personal nature regarding any of th e officers, staff, or employees of the Association or its members, information shared in the course of Board or other Association meetings or discussions (unless such information has been specifically cleared for public release), and any other information which might be reasonably foreseen to be harmful if disclosed outside of the Association and/or for reasons other than in accordance with a D irector’s, Officer’s, or Staff Member ’s duties to the Association. In the case that the Recipient is unclear or has any question regarding whether or not a particular piece of information falls within Confidential Data, the Recipient will consult with the Executive Director or Office Manager prior to disclosing such information.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Confidential Data' within the definition section, after previously using 'Confidential Information' throughout the document, and without making all needed corresponding changes, now makes 'Confidential Information' undefined. Since other section reference 'Confidential Information', it creates confusion with the new definition which refers to 'Confidential Data'. This alters the scope of what is protected, creating potential for disputes about which definition applies in different scenarios.

(6) that in the event Recipient violates any part of this Confidentiality and Non-Disclosure Understanding, the Association may pursue legal and/or equitable remedies from Recipient; (7) that in the event any proceeding to enforce this Confidentiality and Non-Disclosure Understanding or decide any matter relating to it Recipient becomes necessary, the Agreement shall be governed by the laws of Switzerland pertaining thereto and the Recipient agrees to submit to the non-exclusive jurisdiction of the courts of the Canton of Geneva, Switzerland; perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Confidential Information.' The changed text redefines it as 'Confidential Data.' The contradicted text relies on the original definition of 'Confidential Information.' By changing the term in the initial definition without updating all instances throughout the document, the contradicted section now refers to a term that is no longer clearly defined, creating a contradiction because the document now inconsistently uses 'Confidential Information' and 'Confidential Data' leading to ambiguity and potential disputes, which is a misaligned terminology contradiction.
